Interior Book Layout + Ebook Formatting:.d: About the book I am a self-publishing author preparing a nonfiction book called It Takes a Valley for an August 2026 release. The book is written for entrepreneurial ecosystem builders, practitioners in communities across the United States who design programs, build networks, and support entrepreneurs. It blends narrative storytelling with practical frameworks and is approximately 70,000 words. The book will be published in three formats: trade paperback (6x9), hardcover, and ebook (EPUB). It will be distributed through IngramSpark for print and wide distribution, Amazon KDP for print and Kindle, and Draft2Digital for wide ebook distribution. The manuscript will be fully copy edited and locked before it is handed off for layout. You will receive a clean, final Google Doc with no tracked changes. What I need I am looking for an experienced book interior designer and ebook formatter to handle the full production workflow from final manuscript to distribution-ready files. This is a text-heavy nonfiction book. The design should be clean, readable, and professionally typeset, not decorative. Think University of Chicago Press or Island Press, not a self-help paperback. Scope of work 1. Print interior: trade paperback and hardcover Interior layout in Adobe InDesign for a 6x9 trim size - Design system including: chapter openers, running headers, page numbers, front matter (title page, copyright page, dedication, table of contents, foreword/preface), back matter (acknowledgments, notes, bibliography, index if applicable) - Pull quotes and section breaks styled consistently throughout - Export of print-ready PDF/X-1a for IngramSpark (both paperback and hardcover — same interior, different cover file) - Export of KDP-formatted PDF for Amazon print - One round of revisions after author review of proof copy 2. Ebook formatting - Reflowable EPUB3 file formatted for wide ebook distribution - Validated with EPUBCheck before delivery, zero critical errors - Compatible with Kindle (via KDP conversion), Apple Books, Kobo, and Draft2Digital - Front matter, chapter breaks, and table of contents correctly structured with working hyperlinks - All fonts embedded or converted correctly 3. Proof copy coordination - Provide final files in the correct format and resolution for IngramSpark upload - Available for one round of corrections after I receive and review a physical proof copy Technical requirements - Adobe InDesign CC, not Canva, not Affinity, not Word. InDesign is required for print-ready PDF/X-1a export at IngramSpark specifications. - EPUB3 output, not MOBI, not EPUB2 - IngramSpark file specifications: PDF/X-1a, 300 dpi images, fonts embedded, correct bleed and trim settings - KDP specifications: separate PDF formatted to Amazon's current interior guidelines The manuscript includes - Approximately 70,000 words of text-heavy prose - No complex graphics, charts, or data visualizations - Section pull quotes and occasional callout text - Front matter: title page, copyright, dedication, table of contents, foreword - Back matter: acknowledgments, endnotes or chapter notes, further reading, index (TBD) - Part openers and chapter openers throughout You are a strong fit if - You have typeset at least 5 nonfiction books for print-on-demand distribution and can provide titles and/or sample spreads - You work natively in Adobe InDesign, not a template-based tool - You have exported PDF/X-1a files for IngramSpark before and understand their technical specifications - You have produced validated EPUB3 files and routinely run EPUBCheck - You understand the difference between a print PDF and a Kindle-ready file - You can provide a sample layout of 3 to 5 pages using my manuscript before the project begins, this is a condition of hiring You are not a fit if - You use Canva, Vellum templates, or other drag-and-drop tools as your primary production workflow - You cannot provide IngramSpark-ready PDF/X-1a files - Your portfolio is primarily fiction, children's books, or heavily illustrated titles - You cannot validate EPUB files with EPUBCheck Budget and timeline - Manuscript handoff: approximately mid-June 2026 (after copy editing is complete) - Proof copy review period: July 2026 - Final files needed: August 2026 publication - Quote the sample layout (3 to 5 pages) separately; this is a paid sample, not spec work Please quote a fixed price for the full scope: print interior (paperback + hardcover), KDP PDF, and EPUB3 I am not looking for the lowest bid. I am looking for clean, professional work delivered on time. Please price your work at your standard professional rate and include a realistic timeline. What to include in your proposal - Two to three nonfiction book titles you have typeset for print-on-demand; include whether they were distributed through IngramSpark or KDP - Sample spreads or a portfolio link showing interior layout work for text-heavy nonfiction - Confirmation that you work in Adobe InDesign CC and can export PDF/X-1a - Your process for EPUB validation: do you run EPUBCheck as a standard step? - Your fixed-price quote for the full scope described above - Your availability for a June 2026 manuscript handoff and August 2026 final file delivery - Your quote for a paid sample layout of 3 to 5 pages using my manuscript Proposals that do not address these seven points will not be reviewed. I am specifically looking for a specialist in nonfiction print-on-demand production, not a generalist designer.

Animation Style Clean 2D motion graphics — conceptual and story-driven Visual language: networks, layers, momentum, capability — not dashboards, profiles, or UI screens Reference points for motion quality and aesthetic: Linear, Attio, Stripe (systems/network visualisation), Canva (colour and energy) Confident, fluid transitions — not hyperactive fast-cuts, not slow corporate pacing No character-driven scenes, no whiteboard animation, no stock character packs The product UI appears briefly in one beat only — it is never the hero Brand Colours: Royal Blue #3B3DCC, Purple #7B2FBE, Teal #2ABFBF, Amber #F5A623, White Typography: Clean modern sans-serif (Inter, DM Sans or similar) — bold and punchy No serif fonts What We're Providing Finished professional voiceover (MP3/WAV) Full script with beat-by-beat creative direction (11 beats mapped to the VO) Brand assets: logo (SVG/PNG), colour hex codes Detailed creative brief covering visual direction, tone, pacing, and right vs wrong visual choices What We're NOT Looking For Templated transitions that look like generic Fiverr explainers UI-heavy or product-demo style animation Whiteboard or hand-drawn styles Smiling founders pointing at charts Any live-action or stock footage elements Deliverables MP4 (H.264), 1080p minimum (1920×1080), 16:9 Editable project file Production Process The job follows four stages with sign-off at each: Mood Board → Storyboard → Sample Animation (5–10 seconds to validate style and motion quality) → Full Production. Do not proceed between stages without written approval. To Apply Please include 2–3 portfolio samples of motion graphics work in a similar style — abstract, conceptual, B2B preferred. Build 4 Reusable CapCut Templates for Sh.t: Build 4 Reusable CapCut Templates for Short-Form Skincare Content (Swappable Placeholders)
Build 4 Reusable CapCut Templates for Sh.d: I'm a cosmetic chemist launching a skincare brand and posting short-form video daily (TikTok / Reels / Shorts). I need four REUSABLE CapCut templates built — not finished videos. The distinction matters and is the whole job: I'm hiring you to build the empty "machine," where every clip, photo, and text is a labelled SWAPPABLE PLACEHOLDER. Effects must live on the track (not welded to individual clips) so I can drop my own footage in later and keep all timing, transitions, and animations intact. You'll build with placeholder/stock media; I refill them myself for dozens of posts. I have a detailed brief with a frame-by-frame event timeline for each format, a burned-in caption style, and clear acceptance criteria. I've already thought hard about pacing and structure — but I want someone who knows execution better than I do and will share ideas. THE FOUR TEMPLATES: 1. A layered talking-head format (text + face + photo overlays in separate zones) 2. A numbered-list format with a PERSISTENT ANIMATED COUNTER (0/3 to 3/3) 3. A 6-SECOND SEAMLESS LOOP (last frame matches the first, no visible restart) 4. A higher-intensity format with ACCELERATING CUTS synced to a music dynamic shift WHAT YOU'LL DELIVER (per template): - The editable CapCut project file (not a flat MP4, not a locked one-tap template) - A short screen-recording showing how to swap placeholders without breaking anything - A list of SFX/music/assets used - An exported MP4 of the placeholder build FORMAT: vertical 1080x1920, burned-in captions (small native-TikTok style), black & white text for now. BUDGET: Fixed-price package for all four. I'll start with one small PAID test build (a single section of one template) so we both know the reusable approach works before committing to the full set. Please quote the package and the test separately. YOU'RE A FIT IF: you've built genuinely reusable templates before (not just edited one-off videos), you're comfortable with keyframed effects on tracks, and ideally you've done an animated counter or a seamless loop. TO APPLY: Skip the generic pitch. In your first line, tell me: have you built a reusable CapCut template with labelled swappable placeholders before, and how would you approach the seamless loop (last frame = first frame) so it survives a clip swap? Applications that don't answer this will be passed over. Portfolio examples of fast-paced beauty/skincare or UGC short-form are a big plus.

Luxury Packaging Designer — Hyperrealist.t: Luxury Packaging Designer — Hyperrealistic Beauty Subscription Box
Luxury Packaging Designer — Hyperrealist.d: We're creating a beauty subscription box that features multiple brands in every delivery, and we're looking for a designer to create the packaging ... the physical box, structure, materials, and finishes ... at a true luxury level. The gold standard we keep coming back to is Gucci: clean, confident, impeccably finished, and luxurious without trying too hard. That's the feeling we want to capture and make our own. For clarity, this is a packaging design project. We're hiring someone to design a beautiful, premium box — its form, its structure, its materials, and how it presents multiple beauty products inside. A note on who we're looking for: We're specifically looking for designers with beauty packaging experience. Beauty is its own world — the aesthetics, the materials, the way products are showcased — and we've found it really does take someone who has worked in this space to get it right. If your background is in beauty or cosmetics packaging, we'd love to hear from you. If it isn't, this likely isn't the right fit, and that's okay. The packaging we're after We want a box that feels unmistakably luxury from the outside in. Here's what matters most to us: -A refined, premium exterior. A box that signals luxury before it's even opened — beautiful proportions, clean lines, impeccable finish. -Elevated materials and finishes. Smooth, soft, modern luxury. (A note on taste: we're not fans of embossing or hot foil stamping, so please lean away from that look.) -Thoughtful interior structure. Inserts and dividers that hold each brand's product securely and present them like they matter — organized, elegant, with room to breathe rather than crowded. -Considered details. A satisfying closure, a ribbon pull, a branded seal, a thoughtful card — small touches that make the whole thing feel intentional and polished. -A premium opening. The box should open in a way that feels special — a clean, satisfying reveal of the products inside. Throughout, we're drawn to restraint: beautiful materials and elegant finishes over clutter or heavy ornamentation. What you'll be designing toward: We'll provide our vision and reference imagery for the look and feel. The designer we award the project will take that direction and develop an original design — so we want someone who can elevate inspiration into something distinctly their own. You have real creative latitude here; we're giving you the feeling, not a rulebook. Deliverables — a collaborative, two-phase process: The project moves in two phases: Phase 1 — Concepts. At least three distinct design directions (storyboards, mockups, concept boards — whatever suits your process), each a different take on the packaging. We'd love a short walkthrough of your thinking with each one: the choices behind the structure, the materials and finishes, how the products are arranged inside, the closure and opening, and any special touches you'd build in (a magnetic close, a ribbon pull, a hidden compartment, an interior video screen, and so on). We'll review together, share feedback, and approve a direction to take forward. Phase 2 — Final renders. Once we've aligned on a concept, you'll produce the finished hyperrealistic imagery: -3 hyperrealistic images of the box closed -3 hyperrealistic images of the box open, showing the interior structure and how the multi-brand products are presented inside These final images should read as real product photography — photorealistic renders, not flat mockups. To apply, please include: Examples of beauty packaging you've designed (portfolio, past concepts, or relevant work). Please note, food & beverage, apparel, etc will not be considered samples will not be considered. If this sounds like you, we'd love to see your work.

Jewelry CAD Rendering Asset Specialist f.t: Jewelry CAD Rendering Asset Specialist for Modular Bracelet Platform
Jewelry CAD Rendering Asset Specialist f.d: Important Budget Note: The listed USD 200 budget is for the initial paid jewelry CAD rendering test only. This is not the full budget for the complete TENLUMA jewelry rendering project. If the paid test result is successful, we may discuss a larger follow-up project separately. We are looking for a jewelry CAD rendering specialist for a premium modular bracelet platform. Important: This is NOT a generic industrial product rendering job. This is NOT an architecture visualization job. This is NOT a general 3D modeling job. This is NOT AI image generation. We are specifically looking for someone with strong jewelry CAD rendering experience. The right candidate should understand how to render small jewelry components with realistic metal, diamond, gemstone, prong, and stone-setting details. About the project: We are developing TENLUMA, a premium modular bracelet platform. The bracelet is created from multiple small jewelry parts/components. We need clean, high-quality CAD-based rendering assets that can be used inside the platform. This role is for rendering jewelry CAD parts into consistent, platform-ready images. What we need: - High-quality jewelry rendering from CAD files - Realistic white metal / silver / gold material rendering - Natural and refined diamond / gemstone appearance - Accurate preservation of the original CAD geometry and proportions - Consistent camera angle, lighting, scale, centering, and rendering style across multiple parts - Clean transparent PNG output for platform use Very important: We are not looking for generic marketing renders only. We need standardized jewelry rendering assets that can be used consistently across a modular jewelry system. This means every part must follow the same visual standard: same scale, same camera, same lighting, same material quality, same centerline, and clean transparent background. Preferred experience: - Jewelry CAD rendering experience - Rhino / Matrix / MatrixGold / KeyShot or similar jewelry workflow - Experience rendering small jewelry parts - Experience with fine prongs, stone houses, diamond settings, polished metal surfaces, and small luxury jewelry components - Understanding of how jewelry rendering differs from general industrial product rendering - Experience creating transparent PNG product assets with clean alpha edges Initial Paid Test Scope: The USD 200 budget is for this paid test only. Paid test scope: - 2 jewelry parts - 4 transparent PNG renders total For each part: 1. Straight top-view transparent PNG 2. Angled transparent PNG, approximately 35–40 degrees The top-view PNG will be used for straight modular bracelet assembly. The angled PNG will be used as a supporting preview image. Rendering requirements: - Clean transparent background - Premium realistic jewelry material appearance - Exact CAD geometry and proportion preservation - Realistic white metal / silver appearance - Realistic diamond / gemstone sparkle - No excessive blue CG diamond effect - No plastic-looking metal - No rough or white PNG edges - Consistent scale, camera, lighting, and material settings across both parts Please apply only if: - You have real jewelry rendering experience - You can show jewelry-specific rendering samples - You understand the difference between jewelry asset rendering and general industrial product rendering - You can maintain strict consistency across multiple jewelry part renders Applications without jewelry-specific rendering samples may not be considered. We want to create a custom cast-metal wedding gift for the bride and groom. The concept is a miniature investment-banking “deal tombstone” for their marriage, since both members of the couple work in banking. The final piece should look like a small luxury bronze/brass desk object. It will have a base, two upright playing-card plaques, and a central crest. The base text should say: [Groom’s Name] and [Bride’s Name] Merger Closed September 6, 2026 The two playing cards should be a King and Queen of Hearts. The King card represents the groom and should include a low raised portrait of him, probably wearing a suit. He is half Korean and half Jewish, so we would like the border/background of his card to include subtle Korean and Jewish design elements, such as Korean flag trigrams, taegeuk-style geometry, a Star of David, or related geometric details. The Queen card represents the bride. The assembled piece should look seamless from the front. The final assembled object can be roughly desk-paperweight size, maybe around 100–130 mm wide, 70–95 mm tall, and 30–45 mm deep, but we are flexible. The most important thing is that each individual part is small enough and practical enough to cast in a small jewelry investment casting setup. Parametric Jewelry Definitions in Grassh.i: Hourly Intermediate Est. time: Less than 1 month, Less than 30 hrs/week
Parametric Jewelry Definitions in Grassh.d: Brief: Parametric Jewelry Definitions in Grasshopper for ShapeDiver Overview I'm building a web application that generates manufacturing-ready jewelry CAD from customer inputs. The app drives ShapeDiver-hosted Grasshopper definitions via the ShapeDiver backend API. I need you to build parametric jewelry definitions in Grasshopper that I upload to ShapeDiver, which produce castable, watertight jewelry geometry from a set of named parameters. You focus only on the Grasshopper/ShapeDiver definitions — all the app/website/API integration is handled on my side. What to build Phase 1 — start with ONE as a small paid test, then the rest after I approve it: Solitaire engagement ring ← build this first (the pilot) Halo ring Three-stone ring Pavé / eternity band Solitaire pendant Hard requirements (EVERY definition) Built in Grasshopper, fully ShapeDiver-compatible — uploads and computes on ShapeDiver with no errors. Use the ShapeDiver plugin components for outputs/materials. (Peacock plugin is welcome.) Fully parametric — every key dimension driven by a named input parameter (slider / value list). Units = millimetres. Use standard US ring sizes for rings. Manufacturing-ready geometry (this is the most important part): The metal must be ONE watertight, single connected solid (castable — no separate floating pieces). Stones as separate geometry, properly seated and physically held by the metal — prongs/bezel actually grip the stone's girdle, with correct gem seats. No stones floating on top. Castable: respect minimum wall thickness (≈0.8 mm), no paper-thin spurs, prongs strong enough to hold. Outputs — name them exactly: metal → the metal as glTF/GLB (and a STEP export too if possible, for manufacturing). gems → the stones as glTF/GLB. (Kept separate so the app applies metal vs gem materials.) Input parameters — use these EXACT names (My app sets parameters by name, so the names matter. If you must use different names, that's fine — just send me the exact final parameter list for each definition.) Solitaire ring: RingSize — number, US size (3–13) BandWidthMM — number (mm) BandThicknessMM — number (mm) BandProfile — list: round / comfort_fit / knife_edge / flat StoneShape — list: round / oval / princess / cushion / emerald / pear / marquise StoneWidthMM — number (mm) — stone diameter / width StoneLengthMM — number (mm) — for non-round cuts (= width for round) ProngCount — integer (e.g. 4 or 6) ProngStyle — list: round / claw / v_tip SettingHeightMM — number (mm) — head height above the band MetalType — list: yellow_gold / white_gold / rose_gold / platinum / silver The other 4 reuse the above, plus: Halo: HaloStoneCount (int), HaloStoneSizeMM (mm) Three-stone: SideStoneShape (list), SideStoneWidthMM (mm) Eternity band: StoneCount (int), StoneSizeMM (mm), FullEternity (yes/no) Pendant: BailHeightMM (mm), BailThicknessMM (mm) — plus the solitaire stone params We'll finalise each definition's exact parameter list together. Deliverables (per definition) The .gh file. Uploaded to a model in my ShapeDiver account (I'll give you access) — or sent to me to upload. The exact list of parameter names, types, and min/max ranges. A one-line confirmation that it outputs a watertight metal solid with seated stones. Process Solitaire first, as a small paid milestone — so we both confirm it works on ShapeDiver and looks + manufactures correctly. After I approve the Solitaire → you build the other four. To confirm you're the right fit, please reply with: A jewelry piece you've built in Grasshopper (ideally one running on ShapeDiver), and Confirmation you can deliver watertight, castable geometry driven by named parameters. Looking forward to working together — let's start with the Solitaire.

Need a reusable 3D polos template create.t: Need a reusable 3D polos template created
Need a reusable 3D polos template create.i: Hourly Expert Est. time: 1 to 3 months, Less than 30 hrs/week
Need a reusable 3D polos template create.d: CLO 3D Apparel Mockup Automation Project We are a custom apparel company looking to streamline our design process using CLO 3D. Project Goal We want to create reusable CLO 3D garment templates that allow our team to upload a seamless repeat pattern and automatically generate realistic 3D apparel mockups. Current Process Today, our designers manually apply custom patterns to polos and other garments, adjust logo placements, and create customer presentation mockups. We would like to automate as much of this process as possible. Desired Workflow Upload a seamless repeat pattern image (PNG, JPG, or SVG) Upload a customer logo Select garment color(s) Automatically generate realistic 3D mockups Garments Initially we would like templates for: Men's Performance Polo Women's Performance Polo Quarter Zip Lightweight Hoodie Output The system should generate: Front view Back view Side/angled view High-resolution presentation images Additional Requirements Ability to quickly swap patterns and logos Consistent logo placement locations Ability to create multiple colorways Ownership of all source files and templates End Goal Our objective is to reduce mockup creation time from 20–30 minutes per design to just a few minutes while maintaining high-quality customer presentation images. If you have experience with CLO 3D, apparel visualization, automation workflows, or custom apparel design systems, please include examples of similar work.

Signature Custom Smock Design||Seeking a.d: Seeking a fashion/apparel designer to create a signature garment called: The Celebrator Smock™ This is NOT a chef apron. It is NOT a traditional artist smock. It is NOT a tunic or costume. It is a modern host garment designed for people who create experiences, welcome others, cook, travel, gather, host, tell stories, and celebrate life. The garment should feel equally appropriate: * preparing a meal * hosting a dinner party * shopping at a market * arranging flowers * teaching a ritual * traveling through a city * documenting culture * gathering with friends ⸻ Design Objectives Create a silhouette that is: * Modern * Timeless * Gender-inclusive * Comfortable * Sophisticated * Instantly recognizable ⸻ Desired Design References Influences may include: * Japanese workwear smocks * Moroccan djellaba construction details * Scandinavian simplicity * Artist studio garments * Market vendor garments * Contemporary hospitality uniforms IMPORTANT: The final garment should NOT look culturally specific or costume-like. Silhouette Garment Type Pull-over host smock Length Mid-thigh Fit Relaxed Comfortable Designed for movement Shape Slightly architectural Clean lines Modern drape Not oversized Not fitted ⸻ Neckline Signature Funnel Neck Soft standing collar Not a turtleneck Not a chef collar Not a mandarin collar Should frame the face elegantly One of the garment’s defining features ⸻ Closure Side Tie Closure Asymmetrical Located near the shoulder/collar area Thin tie Approximately half the width of traditional apron ties The act of tying should feel ritualistic and intentional Almost like wrapping a gift ⸻ Signature Tie Design The tie is a key design element. Create interchangeable tie options inspired by: Collection A Ancient African textile motifs Inspired by: * mudcloth geometry * Adinkra symbolism * handwoven traditions * indigo resist patterns Avoid literal cultural reproduction. Interpret motifs in a contemporary luxury way. ⸻ Pocket Design The garment must include: Primary Pocket One oversized asymmetrical pocket Large enough to hold: * recipe cards * notebooks * flowers * market purchases * keepsakes Pocket should feel intentional rather than utilitarian. ⸻ Hidden Pocket Interior keepsake pocket Designed to hold: * handwritten note * memory card * heirloom token * personal object This hidden pocket is important to the storytelling of the brand. ⸻ Side Slits Generous side slits for movement Allow: * walking * cooking * sitting * hosting Should contribute to elegant drape ⸻ Sleeves 3/4 sleeve or roll-tab sleeve Designed to roll naturally Should look equally good rolled or unrolled ⸻ Signature Construction Detail Instead of a logo, create a recognizable construction feature. Potential directions: Option A Center-back “Celebration Pleat” Option B Signature stitch line Option C Distinctive seam architecture The goal is brand recognition without branding. ⸻ Materials Preferred: * Linen * Cotton-linen blends * Washed cotton * Lightweight canvas * Garment-dyed fabrics Desired qualities: * Soft * Breathable * Durable * Ages beautifully Avoid: * Performance fabrics * Shiny materials * Heavy synthetics Initial Color Collection Indigo Flagship color Tie inspired by African textile traditions ⸻ Fuchsia Bold expressive version Tie in black or deep charcoal ⸻ Oxblood / Cranberry Cherry Rich celebratory tone Muted tonal tie ⸻ Amethyst Deep jewel tone Subtle lavender accent ⸻ Orange + Peach Two-tone edition Orange exterior Peach interior collar, placket accents, and sleeve reveals ⸻ Black Collector edition Featuring special tie inspired by Caribbean celebration traditions ⸻ Charcoal Grey Light grey tie or a tie and accents inspired by Caribbean Sea ⸻ Deliverables Requested 1. Initial concept sketches (minimum 6) 2. Front view 3. Back view 4. Side view 5. Construction details 6. Fabric recommendations 7. Technical flats 8. Measurement specifications 9. Colorway renderings 10. Production-ready tech pack (preferred) 11. Sample Set of smock in up to seven colorways ⸻ Success Criteria When someone sees this garment, they should not think: “I am about to cook.” They should think: “I am about to gather, host, create, and celebrate.”

Logo Designer for Quiet-Luxury Equestria.d: Job Opportunity: Logo Designer for Quiet-Luxury Equestrian Apparel Brand Job Description: We are seeking a talented graphic designer with expertise in brand identity to develop a sleek, minimalist primary logo and a refined emblem for a new equestrian apparel brand. The brand aesthetic is rooted in quiet luxury and understated elegance—think clean typography, classic styling, and timeless appeal. We want an identity that feels heritage-driven yet modern. Key Deliverables: 1. Primary Logo: Clean, minimalist typography/wordmark for the brand name. 2. Brand Emblem/Icon: A sophisticated monogram or a subtle, modern nod to classic heraldry. (Note: This emblem must be highly scalable and optimised for physical apparel manufacturing. It needs to look flawless when engraved on metal zipper pulls, stamped into buttons, or embroidered directly onto performance fabrics.) 3. Mini Brand Guide: Primary/secondary colour palette specification and typography/font selections. 4. Final Files: Complete package of production-ready Vector files (.AI, .EPS, .SVG) and high-res .PNG layouts. About You: - Have a proven portfolio demonstrating experience with premium, luxury, or high-end minimalist visual identities. - Understand the spatial restrictions of apparel manufacturing (you know how to design lines that won't vanish or blur when stitched or cast in metal). - Direct experience with fashion or equestrian branding is a major plus, but not required if your luxury aesthetic aligns. To apply, please share a link to your portfolio highlighting 2–3 relevant luxury or minimalist brand identities you have built.

Graphic Designer for a New Apparel Line.i: Hourly: $50.00 - $69.00 Expert Est. time: 1 to 3 months, Less than 30 hrs/week
Graphic Designer for a New Apparel Line.d: Caddy Moving is looking for an experienced graphic designer to take our vision and bring it to life. Our goal is to create an EPIC apparel line for movers who work at Caddy. This apparel line will be exclusively offered to those who have worked with us, not just those who sign up on the app. Have you ever heard of a brand that requires you to work for them before you get access to the merch? That's what we're going to do. We're aiming to build a brand identity and spark a love and comradery for the company. Movers will WANT TO WORK to get access to what we're selling. We take inspiration from brands like Ghost Lifestyle who launch street-style clothing and have been wildly successful and building a "brand" in the fitness industry. We want to do the SAME in the moving industry, and that starts with creating clothing that the team actually wants to wear. This is NOT your average moving company. Caddy is a nationwide moving labor company that has over 10,000+ independent movers working local moving jobs on its app. Our movers are: - 18-35 years old - Predominantly male - Enjoy things like sports, hip hop, and Nike Apparel line ideas: - Mainly T-shirts and long-sleeve shirts - Snapback / baseball hats - Hoodies in the winter Our target first launch is June, 2026! We're looking for someone we can build a real RELATIONSHIP with and be our go-to person for OWNING this apparel line. We're targeting 4-5 launches per year with new drops that get our movers EXCITED about working at Caddy. What we want from our ideal person: - Experienced AF in graphic design, specifically apparel - A love for building brand identity and seeing the vision - Local to Chicago so we can talk top-secret ideas in person (not required, but would be a huge plus) If this is you, please reach out! Much Love, Zach, Owner of Caddy Moving

Potential for ongoing work if the relationship is a good fit. **WHAT I WILL PROVIDE** AI-generated concept images Design references Product mockups Brand direction and feedback The concepts are already defined. I am looking for a designer who can professionally recreate and improve them for commercial production. **DELIVERABLES** Source Files (Required). For each design, please provide: --Adobe Illustrator (.AI) --SVG Additional vector formats such as EPS are welcome but not required. Production Files For each design, please provide: --High-resolution PNG with transparent background --Print-ready PDF --Production Artwork Requirements Artwork should: --Be professionally recreated from the supplied concept images --Eliminate AI artifacts and inconsistencies --Use clean typography, spacing, and alignment --Scale cleanly without loss of quality --Be suitable for DTG printing --Be suitable for screen printing if needed --Use commercially licensed fonts and assets --Follow professional apparel design standards PNG exports should be suitable for Printful, Printify, and other major POD platforms. Apparel artwork should generally be delivered at 4500 × 5400 pixels (or larger) at 300 DPI. **BRAND STYLE** The brand combines: --Americana --Patriotic themes --Vintage athletic club aesthetics --Humorous and satirical messaging --Health and fitness culture --Lifestyle apparel Many designs are typography-driven, vintage-inspired badges, athletic club logos, seals, and simple graphic treatments rather than highly detailed illustrations. Examples of themes include: Raw Milk Athletic Club Department of Seed Oil Defense Naturally Enhanced Freedom Porch **WORKFLOW** --I provide concept images and references. --Designer recreates and improves the artwork. --Initial proof submitted. --Revisions based on feedback on initial proof. --Final files delivered. **OWNERSHIP** Upon final payment, I will own full commercial rights to all final artwork and source files created for this project. **TO APPLY** Please include: --Examples of apparel graphics you have designed. --Examples of print-on-demand work. --Examples of typography-focused merchandise designs. --Your estimated project cost. --Your estimated turnaround time. --Confirmation that you can provide editable vector source files. Please also answer the following question: --What is your process for converting AI-generated apparel mockups into professional vector artwork suitable for commercial printing? **PREFERRED EXPERIENCE** The ideal candidate has experience with: --Apparel brands --Printful --Printify --Shopify --Vintage Americana design --Athletic club / collegiate-style graphics --Merchandise and POD production Before awarding the full project, I may select 2–3 finalists and pay each designer to complete one test design. The designer who best matches the brand aesthetic and quality expectations will be offered the remainder of the project.

We develop thoughtful products, stories, and experiences with a strong point of view. Our first in house apparel capsule focuses on material discipline, clean structure, breathability, and pricing integrity. This is not trend driven fashion. This is not synthetic fast fashion. This is not oversized loungewear pretending to be design. We are building elevated essentials with shape, function, and restraint. Think structured comfort, breathable natural fabrics, and wearable pieces that feel considered without feeling precious. The Role We are looking for a freelance apparel designer to help shape the first MÙA Form capsule collection. You will work with a clear brand concept and help translate it into product through silhouette development, fabric direction, technical sketches, and early design development. This role is best suited for someone who can move between concept and execution. You should have taste, but also know how garments actually come together. What You’ll Work On Translate mood boards and brand direction into strong, wearable silhouettes Develop and refine a small capsule collection Create technical flats and design sketches Guide fabric and material direction, with preference for breathable natural or natural blend fabrics Refine fit intent, proportions, garment function, and construction details Help define the visual and product language of the brand Support early development thinking so designs can be handed off cleanly for next stage execution What We’re Looking For Experience in apparel design and garment construction Strong understanding of silhouette, fit, fabric, and proportion Ability to turn creative direction into practical, wearable product Strong eye for restraint, balance, and elevated essentials Comfortable working collaboratively and taking direction Experience with technical sketches and early product development Bonus if you have experience with fabric sourcing, factory communication, or tech pack development This Role Is Probably Not a Fit If Your work leans heavily into occasionwear, costume, or trend fashion You focus only on concept and do not think through construction or wearability You are primarily a stylist, graphic designer, or creative director without apparel development experience To Apply, please submit: Short introduction Relevant project examples, especially capsule collections or elevated basics Please include your estimated pricing for an initial phase covering concept refinement, silhouette development, fabric direction, and technical sketches for a small capsule. Clothing Designer for Men & Women's Wear.t: Clothing Designer for Men & Women's Wear
Clothing Designer for Men & Women's Wear.d: Shangri-La Capsule Apparel Collection — Fall & Spring 2027 Project Snapshot Client: Shangri-La — vertically integrated cannabis retailer (Connecticut, Ohio, Missouri) Engagement: Fixed-price, not to exceed $10,000 total Deliverable: A production-ready apparel design system for a branded retail capsule — sold in-store and offered as loyalty-program redemption Markets: Connecticut, Ohio, Missouri. Every design must be compliant and sellable across all three states with no rework. We will not produce single-state SKUs. Seasons: Fall 2027 and Spring 2027 drops, designed together in this engagement Objective Design a cohesive, production-ready capsule across two seasonal drops (Fall and Spring) that: Reflects the Shangri-La brand — purple-and-black forward, premium and confident, with slight reference to pink in intentional places. Spans everyday staples and limited-edition hero pieces Hands cleanly to manufacturers and decorators (vectors, digitized embroidery, tech packs) Works across CT, OH and MO without modification Aesthetic & Competitive References Higher Stitch — cannabis-space capsule collections; elevated, comfort-led positioning (their own line explicitly rejects cartoon graphics and cheap tees — exactly our direction) Jeeter / Jeeter Apparel — limited-edition drop strategy, hype cadence and premium apparel capsules Supreme — streetwear drop model; clean branded staples alongside statement pieces Direction: elevated streetwear. Clean, confident, wearable. We want both simple, logo-driven everyday pieces and a few artistic, statement-level designs that can be featured on aspects like jerseys or higher-priced pieces like hats (for moments like Superbowl/football season)Å. Approved direction (from our prior internal round — reference deck available on award) Clean logo / script lockups featuring the hummingbird (e.g. retro “Good Vibes,” “Paradise Found”) Tasteful tonal / textured all-over prints Bold typographic treatments and graphic statements that stay brand-forward Avoid (rejected in prior round and on compliance grounds) Mascots or character illustrations of any kind — do not design these Any depiction of consumption — no lit joints, smoke, paraphernalia in use Heavy cannabis-leaf-forward iconography (skulls, leaf “beards,” etc.) Busy, cluttered all-over doodle prints Anything that reads juvenile, chaotic or generic Quantity (per garment) 4 design concepts each 2 colorways each, anchored in purple/black Note on scope vs. budget (please read): Full literal scope (≈9 garment types × 4 designs × 2 colorways + production files + tech packs) is ambitious for a $10K cap. We are open to — and encourage — a cohesive “core design system” approach: a set of lead concepts per season that flex across multiple garments, plus garment-specific treatments. In your proposal, recommend the structure that delivers the strongest, most cohesive collection within budget, and flag any trade-offs. Application methods (specify the right method per design) Screen print — everyday tees, sweats Embroidery — hats, beanies, hoodies, left-chest hits Woven / PVC patches — hats, hoodies, flannel All-over dye-sublimation — jerseys, statement pieces Tackle twill / appliqué — jerseys, varsity-style hits You should recommend the best application per piece and deliver files accordingly. Deliverables For every design: Production-ready vector artwork — .AI + .EPS + print-ready .PDF, fully layered, in CMYK + Pantone with exact brand color callouts (HEX / CMYK / Pantone) Screen-print color separations where applicable Embroidery digitized files — .DST / .EMB with thread color callouts (Madeira / Isacord) Tech packs for all cut-and-sew pieces (jerseys, flannel, crop tees): flat sketches (front/back), graded measurements (POMs), materials, trims/labels, colorways, decoration placement + method, bill of materials Mockups — front/back, both colorways, shown on-garment Master color / spec sheet for the full collection File handoff: organized, clearly named folders by season and garment. Ideal Candidate Apparel / streetwear designer with both graphic design and garment / tech-pack experience Portfolio showing decorated apparel (screen print + embroidery) and cut-and-sew / all-over pieces Proven ability to produce manufacturer-ready files (vectors, digitized embroidery, tech packs) Bonus: cannabis or other regulated-industry apparel experience Comfortable working within strict brand guidelines and a structured, milestone-based review process Hard deadline: all final, manufacturer-ready files (including tech packs) are due by the end of August 2026 so Shangri-La can place the production order in September 2026. Production is overseas and must arrive ahead of the Chinese New Year factory shutdown (≈February 6, 2027); the collection then drops at retail in Fall 2027 and Spring 2027.

Bring a childhood memory to life with wa.t: Bring a childhood memory to life with watercolor illustrations, attention to detail, whimsy
Bring a childhood memory to life with wa.i: Hourly: $15.00 - $30.00 Entry Level Est. time: 1 to 3 months, Less than 30 hrs/w
Bring a childhood memory to life with wa.d: This is a children's book told in rhyme. It is based on memories of my grandmother's apartment in the 1970-80's in Chicago, Illinois, USA. The basic concept is that there is a young girl who loves going to her grandmother's place for Friday night dinner. Everyone is welcome there. It is called "One More Chair." The basic concept is that as the evening goes on, more and more people come over for dinner. As they come, and ask if there is room at the table, grandma says, of course, just go get one more chair. The visual gag is the table gets more and more crowded, and the chairs get more and more unmatched, folding chairs, arm chairs, stools, etc. until there are no more chairs! What will happen when the little boy from the upstairs apartment comes down to join? Where will he sit? What will Grandma do??? Here is an example of the first few pages: The title of the book is, “One More Chair” Page 1 Illustration is: Shows Molly skipping down the street towards her grandmother’s apartment building. The sun is just beginning to set. The text is: Every Friday evening, Molly dashed through the air, To Grandma’s warm house full of love everywhere. Page 2 Illustration is: Grandma standing in open doorway welcoming Molly into the apartment. The apartment is on the second floor of the building, so there is a flight of stairs and a little vestibule that is visible where Molly is standing. Behind the Grandma is a long hallway that leads to the dining room. Grandma is wearing a short sleeve brown shirt with white lace design, tan slacks, and flat shoes. Her hair is grey but well-styled and short. She has kind eyes and a nice smile. Text is: The table was set with great food to share, Molly looked forward to her family being there. Page 3: Illustration is: Molly and Grandma are walking down the hallway, there are family pictures on the walls. They walk into the dining room. There is a long, dark wood rectangular table with 4 matching dining room chairs . There are 2 adults already sitting at the table. The table has place settings for 4. Foods such as roasted chicken, baked potatoes, green beans, and a big bowl of green salad , a loaf of rye bread are on the table. There is a poodle dog laying underneath the table and a grey striped cat sitting on the windowsill , a sheer curtain and a few plants on the same window. There is a wooden china cabinet on a wall and some art work on the walls, some still life of fall fruits. The Text is: They’d laugh and they’d chatter, they’d tell stories bright, They’d eat Grandma’s cooking and talk through the night, Everyone knew they were welcome in there, Just find a small space and pull up a chair.

Children's Book Interior Designer & Illu.t: Children's Book Interior Designer & Illustrator (Amazon KDP)
Children's Book Interior Designer & Illu.d: TITLE: Children's Book Interior Designer & Illustrator (Amazon KDP) OVERVIEW We are publishing a full-color illustrated question-and-answer encyclopedia for curious girls aged 6 to 12. We are looking for an experienced book interior designer to take our finished written content and turn it into a polished, print-ready Amazon KDP paperback interior, including all illustrations and imagery. This is a fixed-price project. Our budget is listed in this posting. This project does have the potential to turn into an ongoing 1x1 relationship if done well. WHAT THE BOOK IS - Title: 100,000 Whys for Curious Girls - A friendly, fact-packed encyclopedia answering 500+ "why" and "how" questions across topics like science, space, animals, nature, the human body, and technology. - Audience: girls aged 6 to 12 (and the parents and grandparents who buy it). SPECIFICATIONS - Trim size: 8.5 x 11 inches (US Letter). - Interior: full color (KDP standard color), white paper. - Length: approximately 150 pages (160 max). - Content: about 540 questions and answers across 11 themed chapters. - Page layout: a clean, consistent template of 4 question-and-answer boxes plus 1 "Cool Fact" box per page. - Recurring feature: short illustrated "Curious Girls Who Changed the World" spotlight elements woven through the book. - Front matter (title page, copyright, short intro notes, how-to-use, table of contents) and back matter (topic index, closing page). - A per-chapter color-coding system for easy navigation. SCOPE OF WORK - Design a reusable master page template and overall visual style for the interior. - Typeset all provided text (questions, answers, cool facts, spotlights) into the layout. - Create and source ALL visual content for the book: illustrations, diagrams, and images for every page. You are fully responsible for image sourcing and for ensuring everything is properly licensed for commercial sale on Amazon. All imagery must be cleared for commercial use, with no copyright or stock-license issues. - Build front and back matter, page numbering, and a working table of contents. - Deliver a print-ready interior PDF that meets current Amazon KDP requirements (correct bleed, margins, gutter, embedded fonts). - Deliver the editable source files (InDesign or equivalent), all illustration files, and organized assets with proof of licensing where applicable. STYLE DIRECTION - Modern, bright, and fun, but NOT babyish. We want a smart, dignified look that respects the reader. - Please avoid the generic "pink everything" cliche. Sophisticated and colorful, with strong, sharp imagery. - Large, clean, easy-to-read real text. No blurry, stretched, or low-resolution images, and no text saved as images. - Consistent illustration style throughout the entire book. WHAT WE PROVIDE - All written content, finalized, organized by chapter and page. - A detailed brief covering tone, format, and chapter structure. WHAT IS NOT INCLUDED - The cover. This project is for the interior only. REQUIREMENTS - Fluent or native English. This is essential, since you will be laying out English text for young readers and catching spacing, hyphenation, and line-break issues. - Proven experience designing Amazon KDP paperback interiors. Full-color children's or educational books strongly preferred. - Strong illustration skills and a clear, consistent visual style suitable for children aged 6 to 12. - Solid experience sourcing and licensing commercial-use imagery, and the ability to document those licenses. - A portfolio with relevant samples (please share links or files). - Strong command of KDP print specifications and color interior setup. - Reliable communication and the ability to hit agreed milestones. TO APPLY 1. Start your proposal with the word CURIOUS so we know you read this in full. 2. Share 2 to 3 relevant samples, ideally full-color children's or educational books you both designed and illustrated. 3. Confirm the listed budget works for you, or include your counter-offer and reasoning, along with your estimated timeline. 4. Confirm your English fluency and that you will handle all illustrations and image sourcing, fully licensed for commercial sale. We review every proposal that follows these steps. We look forward to seeing your work.

These are ProRes files, they are large. You need the drive space to handle them without delays. --- Workflow 1. I film and send raw footage with a brief (talking points, planned B-roll, intended length, reference videos) 2. You deliver a rough cut (V1) within 48 hours 3. I send revision notes 4. Final cut delivered within 24 hours of revision notes 5. Repeat weekly (scaling to twice weekly) --- Communication This is non-negotiable. You must be reachable and responsive during working hours. That means: - Respond on Slack within 4 hours during business days. No exceptions. - If you are going to miss a deadline, you tell me before the deadline, not after. - If something is unclear in the brief, you ask immediately. You do not guess and deliver something wrong. - You do not disappear mid-project. If you go silent for 24+ hours during an active edit without explanation, we are done. - Strong English comprehension is required. You need to understand briefs, revision notes, and creative direction without repeated clarification. --- Requirements Must Have: - Based in US, Canada, UK, or EU. EST timezone overlap required. - Portfolio showing long-form talking head YouTube content (not corporate video, not wedding films, not gaming) - Portfolio showing vlog-style content (not just talking head) - Color grading reel or examples showing cinematic grades on log footage - Dedicated and serious about this as ongoing work, not a side project between other gigs - Self-sufficient editor who can take a brief and deliver without hand-holding - Available for ongoing work, not juggling 5+ other clients Do Not Apply If: - You watched the reference videos and you cannot match that quality - Your portfolio is all short-form or horizontal corporate - You only use Premiere for grading (no DaVinci experience) - You ghost when things get busy - You have never edited for a YouTuber before - You cannot commit to a 2-month trial period - Your internet is slow or your storage is limited - You need detailed instructions on every cut, transition, and music choice --- Details - Rate: Fixed price per video (send your rate with your application) - Volume: 4 videos/month to start, scaling to 8 - Payment: Monthly - Start: Immediately - Trial: 2 months, paid --- To apply, answer all five questions. Missing any one will disqualify your application. 1. Go to youtube.com/@definitelytai/videos and watch the Toronto vlog and a talking head video. Tell me one specific thing each video does well from an editing perspective and one thing you would improve. Be specific, not generic. 2. Send your 3 best long-form YouTube edits. At least one must be talking head, at least one must be vlog-style. No reels, no short-form, no corporate. 3. Send 2 examples of your color grading work on log footage. Tell me what software you used. 4. Name a content creator with 100K+ followers you have edited for. What was your role and how long did you work with them? 5. What is your per-video rate for a 15-minute talking head video and a 15-minute vlog? Give me both numbers.

This is a visual systems + technical diagramming job. --- Scope of Work You will design: • A full investor pitch deck (15–18 slides) • Clean, clinical enterprise aesthetic • Technical diagrams (integration flow, data pipeline, system architecture) • Charts and visualizations (data provided; you design the visuals) • A light Figma component system for consistency • Layouts that feel like a $20M Series A company, not a startup template --- What’s Already Done You do not need to create: • Story architecture • Narrative flow • Investor logic • Market framing • Moat articulation • Financial model • Script or voice You are executing, not inventing. --- What We’re Looking For • Figma mastery — components, auto‑layout, spacing discipline • Technical diagram ability — can turn complexity into clarity • Clinical/enterprise aesthetic — clean, minimal, professional • Typography discipline — hierarchy, consistency, restraint • Chart literacy — no Canva charts • Fast iteration — able to keep pace with a fast‑moving founder • Visual pushback — able to say “this layout doesn’t work” If your portfolio is mostly templates, gradients, or startup‑cute styles, this is not a fit. --- Required Portfolio Samples Please include: 1. Freelance Immigration Case Manager / Par.d: About the Role QX Immigration is seeking experienced freelance Case Managers / Paralegals to provide immigration case management and paralegal support on a per-case or per-project independent contractor basis. This is a non-employee, independent contractor engagement, where you retain full discretion over how, when, and where you perform your work, subject only to agreed deliverables, deadlines, and professional standards. We are particularly seeking professionals with direct experience handling O-1B extraordinary ability petitions (artists, entertainers, and creative professionals). This is a long-term opportunity, and we are looking for contractors able to commit for a minimum of one year, with potential for ongoing engagement and increased responsibility over time. Key Responsibilities Depending on assignment scope, responsibilities may include: Conducting case intake reviews and organizing supporting documentation Preparing immigration forms and assembling filing-ready petition packages Drafting support letters, indices, and exhibit lists under attorney supervision Managing client communications related to document collection and procedural updates Maintaining accurate case tracking in internal systems Coordinating filing preparation and post-filing follow-up activities All deliverables, timelines, and expectations will be defined per individual case or project assignment. Compensation Structure (Per-Case Model) This role is structured on a flat-rate per case basis (not hourly). Starting rate: $750–$800 per case Performance-based progression: up to $1,100 per case Rate increases may be awarded based on: Case quality and accuracy Consistency and reliability Efficiency and turnaround time Approval outcomes (where applicable) Seniority and demonstrated expertise Additional Incentives Performance-based bonuses Google review bonuses Discretionary rewards tied to quality and responsiveness Professional Standards & Working Norms These expectations support operational consistency and do not limit your independent contractor status. Systems: Use designated platforms (ClickUp and Docketwise) for all case tracking and updates Responsiveness: Respond to case-related communications within one business day, unless otherwise agreed Availability: Provide advance notice if unavailable for more than 48 hours and ensure case continuity where possible Quality: Follow firm templates, checklists, and attorney direction; revise work promptly when needed Confidentiality: Maintain strict confidentiality and use secure systems at all times Legal Scope: No legal advice may be provided; all work is performed under attorney supervision where required Qualifications Required Demonstrated experience in U.S. immigration case management or paralegal support Strong experience with O-1B petition preparation and/or extraordinary ability cases Familiarity with immigration forms, exhibit organization, and filing-ready petition assembly Strong written communication and client-facing professionalism Ability to manage deadlines independently across multiple cases Compliance with confidentiality and data security requirements Independent contractor status (responsible for own taxes, insurance, and business expenses) Preferred Experience with ClickUp, Docketwise, or similar case management systems Experience drafting support letters, indices, and legal-style documentation Exposure to AI tools or workflow automation in legal operations Strong track record of consistency in attorney-reviewed work environments How to Apply Interested contractors should submit: Current resume Brief summary of relevant immigration case management experience (especially O-1B cases) Availability Experience with case management platforms (ClickUp, Docketwise, etc.)

Executive CV / Founder Resume Specialist.d: I am looking for a senior CV/resume specialist with strong experience in executive CVs, founder profiles, venture-building roles, and premium one-page CV design. The CV is already drafted and structurally complete. I am not looking for someone to create it from scratch. I need someone to refine, sharpen, and professionally polish the existing version so it is more credible, visually balanced, and commercially compelling. The profile is for a GCC-based founder/operator. The CV positions me as Co-Founder and General Manager of MinDev, a UAE-incorporated venture studio operating across Saudi Arabia and the GCC. The work spans AI-enabled software delivery, regulated software platforms, enterprise client engagement, venture incubation, equity-for-services structures, and Saudi market execution. The current CV already includes the key experience, including: * Co-Founder and General Manager of MinDev * Venture studio operations across custom development, internal ventures, and equity-for-services partnerships * Enterprise software and regulated platform work for Saudi-focused markets * Selected engagements involving Daify, a KSA-native property management system for the short-term rental market * Alkhaleej Training & Education / Gordon Ramsay Academy KSA digital platform work * Coordination between Saudi and London-side stakeholders * HyperPay/payment integration, third-party dependencies, go-live readiness, and delivery coordination * Earlier commercial leadership experience in Saudi luxury retail and hospitality-related advisory work * EHL Hospitality Business School education What I need from you: 1. Refine the wording so it sounds senior, polished, and credible without exaggeration. 2. Improve the visual hierarchy, spacing, alignment, typography, and general layout. 3. Keep it to one page if possible. 4. Make it feel premium and executive, but not over-designed or gimmicky. 5. Improve readability for both human readers and professional screening. 6. Make the experience section easier to scan, especially the distinction between roles, selected ventures, and selected engagements. 7. Ensure the wording is commercially sharp and suitable for GCC/Saudi/UAE business environments. 8. Flag any claims, titles, or wording that may sound inflated, unclear, or risky. 9. Recommend small wording improvements where needed, especially around MinDev, Daify, Alkhaleej, and Gordon Ramsay Academy KSA. 10. Deliver an editable version and a polished PDF version. Important: I do not want a generic CV rewrite. I want refinement of an already-developed executive CV. The tone should be founder/operator, venture studio, enterprise software, and GCC-commercial — not a junior job-seeker CV. The final output should make the profile feel sharper, cleaner, more credible, and more boardroom-ready.
